concepts.title: Planning is a Continuous Process
concepts.description: Planning is a continuous process as it requires continuous evaluation and revision to adapt to changing internal and external factors.

concepts.title: Types of Planning
concepts.description: There are two main types of planning: 1) Strategic Planning and 2) Tactical Planning. Strategic Planning focuses on long-term goals, while Tactical Planning focuses on short-term goals.

concepts.category: One of the Most Important Facts
concepts.title: Role of Planning in Business
concepts.description: Planning plays a crucial role in business as it helps in achieving goals, allocating resources, and making informed decisions.

concepts.title: SMCPE Framework
concepts.description: To remember the SMCPE framework for planning, use the mnemonic 'Strategic Methods Control Policies Execution'. S stands for Situation, M for Methods, C for Control, P for Policies, and E for Execution.

concepts.title: Planning Mistake: Underestimating External Factors
concepts.description: Students often make the mistake of underestimating external factors while planning. It's essential to consider both internal and external factors to create an effective plan.

concepts.title: Importance of Planning
concepts.description: Planning is essential for a business as it helps in achieving goals, allocating resources, and making informed decisions, ultimately leading to success.

concepts.category: One of the Most Important Facts
concepts.title: Types of Plans
concepts.description: There are two main types of plans: 1) Business Plan and 2) Action Plan. A Business Plan outlines the overall strategy, while an Action Plan outlines specific steps to be taken.

concepts.title: Planning is a Deliberate Process
concepts.description: Planning is a deliberate process as it requires careful thought and consideration of various factors to achieve business goals.
